explanation: This command uses the -Command parameter instead of -EncodedCommand and omits encoding to test if the detection rule can be bypassed by using a more readable command line.
This command uses the short form of the -Command parameter (-c) and omits quotes to test if the detection rule can be bypassed by using minimal syntax.
This command uses the -Command parameter without quotes or encoding to test if the detection rule can be bypassed by using a simple, unencoded command line.
This command uses the alternative PowerShell executable (pwsh.exe) and the -Command parameter to test if the detection rule can be bypassed by using a different executable name.
This command uses the alternative module name (Microsoft.Exchange.Powershell.Snapin) to test if the detection rule can be bypassed by using a different module name.
